About us :
Hello fellow travelers. Welcome to Travelmoji. We are a community of travel enthusiasts who find solace going around the world. In our sincerest efforts to satisfy your wanderlust, we bring forward insightful content featuring the wonderful places around the world neatly compiled into the ‘Top things to-do lists.

Our vision at Travelmoji is to form a community of like-minded travellers, who share our passion for unique experiences, breathtaking destinations, exploring different cultures, and connecting with people.

videos.transcript: [Music] [Music] casablanca a port city and commercial hub is located in western morocco and fronts the atlantic ocean it is the main gateway to morocco having the main international airport this city is the tourist's first taste of the whole country it is an excellent choice for people who want to experience the present and learn about the past too it is the largest city in morocco and stands at the fifth position of being the most romantic place in the world perfect setting out looking at the moroccan coastline makes it the best place to share a kiss with your loved ones while being the most modern and business-oriented city of morocco casablanca is also a place rich in history it is a perfect travel destination for people who love to connect to other cultures and feel local-like rather than waddling around the city like the tourists welcome to travel travelmoji your exclusive boarding pass to access the world in today's video we will take you on a virtual journey to casablanca and together we will explore the top 10 things to do in casablanca number 10 united nations square if you are planning to take your partner out on a romantic and memorable date this place won't let you down united nations square is a busy central square in casablanca and a good stop during your trip it is a lively place with people from all parts of the world the architecture is adorable and the streets very busy it has everything nearby from restaurants and coffee shops to clothes shoes and accessories there are many picturesque places where you can click hundreds of pictures without getting bored additionally there are also many nightclubs where you can enjoy some drinks the place is totally secured by cops hence you do not have to be worried about getting mugged number 9 park cindy bad park cindy bod is a perfect place to spend a beautiful day with friends and family this is a gem for the city with lots of rides green space a zoo and a lake the entrance fee to this amusement park is relatively very low the rides are mostly for youngsters but there are also still various rides for adults you can also zipline around the park the open air zoo at the back of the park boasts giraffes lions tigers and loads more whereas you can also enjoy boating in the lake but if you are planning to include park zindibad in your bucket list do not forget to keep it on a weekday as during the weekends this place is crowded and you might not be able to enjoy it much [Music] number eight mohammed v square also known as pigeon square this square is named after the former king of morocco mohammed v built in the 19th century by the french this is a lovely place to visit right in the middle of a magnificent history it has quite impressive buildings with magnificent carvings on the doors and exquisite mosques mohammed v square is pretty cool place to chill especially on hot days when you can see pigeons picking food there is a huge fountain and it also contains theater within beautifully maintained you will always find this place a bit crowded in a nutshell it is a nice park located in the heart of casablanca where you can enjoy watching the water fountain pigeons and even theater [Music] number seven dream village dream village is a nice clean place located in a fresh air area for entertainment it is a well-equipped park with various fun activities for everyone whether horse riding or aqua activities this zoo has an impressive collection of animals from all over the world and their customer service is remarkable which makes this place so unique it contains various restaurants as well as a mosque inside if you've missed your morning prayers it is strongly recommended to anyone who is passionate about nature and animals in one word it is a perfect place where you can stay in harmony with nature number six hamam ziyani the moroccan bath known as hamam is a traditional cleansing ritual that is quite popular in the middle east as a purifying treatment for the whole body if you want to feel relaxed and special at the same time this is the most recommended place for you it provides you with a very authentic bath experience not like the usual spas this place is very well maintained and takes hygiene very seriously baths here are gender segregated and you will be attended by a person of your gender so that you can enjoy your time with the utmost comfort the staff inside is very friendly and kind additionally they provide you with lockers to safeguard your personal belongings the whole massage and bath will cost you near around 350 dirhams but the experience that you will gather from this place is totally luxurious number five royal palace also known as habus the royal palace of casablanca is not yet open for tourists but you can take photographs from outside or visit the entrance gate and walk through the gardens being home to the king the palace is very well maintained it is an agricultural masterpiece the markets surrounding the palace sell moroccan culture through their clothes and artifacts there are also many libraries on the side of the palace where you can enjoy some leisure time what makes this palace unique from others is its colorful and golden pattern which is more than admirable [Music] number four arab league park if you are tired of waddling in the city or beaches or markets and want to relax for some time arab league park will not disappoint you this park is absolutely stunning it is located in the center of casablanca and you can visit it without any need for transportation it is very well maintained clean and totally secured all trees are placed and grooved in very tidy order there are various picturesque spots where you can click picks and videos to make your journey memorable you can definitely plan a picnic at this park with your family the swings and separate playing spots will fill your kids with joy and happiness and you can spend some quality time with your partner too [Music] number three les marques solider authentic moroccan goods from all over the country in one place le marche solidaire is one of the most reasonable markets where you can find a bit of everything in the whole of a morocco prices are shockingly low the quality is excellent and the packaging is perfect even for plane travel whether you talk about pottery or shoes baskets beauty or food products or even clothes and carpets this market offers everything most of the products sold here are handmade and shopping from here helps many small scale industries to grow with a wide choice of local products this is one of the best places in casablanca for family shopping respecting the environment and the country's culinary traditions number two anfa park ideally located in the heart of the old district casa anfa which was the old airport anubha park is a unique and necessary part of the city that is destined to soon become one of its landmark experiences this park is a quiet place to breathe some fresh air in casablanca it is a good place with a huge green space where you can also bring your kids to have fun there is a massive playground for kids you can also go there if you want to relax and read a book or just to chill as it has lots of seats and lots of space and grass for you to enjoy there are also many parts within the park where you can practice sports or even work out you can also visit this park if you want to jog or walk in the morning it is totally safe and secured with cameras and cops anfa park is like a perfect combo for tourists and also the residents not only sports and fun activities but this park also contains some picturesque spots like a place with mirrors in the background or the magnificent surrounding with some big towers and buildings some shops are also set up inside the park which is affordable so that you can enjoy your relaxing time while shopping [Music] number one hassan ii mosque hassan ii mosque is an awe-inspiring and majestic structure located in casablanca at the edge of the atlantic ocean it is the second largest functioning mosque and the seventh largest in the world it was built by king hassan ii and artisans from all over morocco were appointed it represents the history and religion of morocco and islam the architecture beautiful colors and view are just breathtaking the structure is gorgeous and we could say that it was clearly built with great care in the best materials it is one of the top visited sites of morocco it has a beautiful ocean view where people can also enjoy the sunset close to the prayer time people are directed towards the turkish bath area to make wudu which is the cleansing ritual before prayers if you wish to spend a peaceful evening this is your place [Music] you

videos.title: Casablanca Morocco: 10 BEST Things To Do In 2024 (Travel Guide)
videos.description:
Casablanca, Morocco's largest city, is a vibrant blend of modernity and tradition. Known for its stunning architecture, the city is home to the iconic Hassan II Mosque, one of the largest and most beautiful mosques in the world, situated on the Atlantic coast. Casablanca's bustling cityscape features a mix of Art Deco and contemporary buildings, with the old medina offering a glimpse into the city's rich history. The city is also Morocco's economic hub, with a lively port and thriving business district. Casablanca's coastal location, cosmopolitan atmosphere, and cultural diversity make it a fascinating and dynamic destination.

Contact us via email: ytmediabrands@gmail.com

#travelscout

videos.transcript: welcome to this complete Casablanca Morocco guide Casablanca the Cosmopolitan heart of Morocco where tradition marries modernity in a dance as vibrant as the city itself it's a city that never sleeps where the aroma of Moroccan tea mingles with the scent of the Atlantic Ocean our journey today will take us through 10 captivating corners of Casablanca we'll explore the Grandeur of the Hassan 2 mosque wander through the Labyrinth of the old Medina and stroll along the glamorous and diab cornich we'll indulge in retail therapy at Morocco mall and Marvel at the Royal Palace of Casablanca our senses will be tantalized at the bustling Central Market and will soak in the charm of habo quarter will admire the gothic beauty of Casablanca Cathedral bask in the historical significance of Muhammad vth square and finally unwind at the iconic Rick's Cafe so buckle up for an unforgettable Journey let's start our countdown with a true Gem of the city at number 10 we have the Majestic Hassan 2 mosque rising from the shores of Casablanca this architectural Marvel is a testament to Morocco's Rich cultural heritage the mosque's Grandeur is or inspiring with its towering minet reaching into the sky the mosque boasts unique features that are Testament to the Ingenuity of Moroccan design its retractable roof is an engineering Marvel transforming the vast prayer Hall into an open air Pavilion at a moment's notice and let's not forget the glass floor of the mosque's lower level a stunning feature that offers a mesmerizing view of the sea below every inch of the mosque is adorned with intricate mosaics carved wood panels and ornate marble pillars reflecting the beauty of traditional Moroccan craftsmanship the hantu mosque isn't just a place of worship but a symbol of Morocco's past present and future this architectural Wonder sets the toone for the Marvels that await Us in Casablanca next up at number nine we have the historic old Medina this original heart of Casablanca is a treasure Trove of winding labyrinth in streets brimming with history and oozing charm it's like a vibrant tapestry of life from a bygone era preserved for the modern Adventurer every nook and cranny of the old Medina tells a story the traditional shops or sus are filled to the brim with colorful spices intricate handicrafts and exquisite textiles that are A Feast for the senses each transaction is a dance of negotiation a centuries old tradition that lives on today the sounds of the Medina are a symphony of Life the call to prayer Echoes through the air mingling with the chatter of locals and the clatter of Artisans at work the scent of freshly baked bread wafts through the narrow Alleyways inviting you to pause and Savor the moment stepping into the Medina is is like stepping back in time coming in at number eight is the vibrant a diab Cornish this is Casa Blanca's Palm dotted beachfront prominade a place where the city's Rhythm Mellows to the lullabi of the lapping Atlantic waves the Cornish is a playground of contrasts where the old world charm of Morocco meets the Contemporary lifestyle here you'll find an array of restaurants offering a smus board of Cuisines to satiate every pallet from traditional Moroccan dishes to International favorites the gastronomic journey here is as diverse as it is delicious and let's not forget the cafes where you can sip a mint tea while watching the world go by as the sun dips below the Horizon andab cornich transforms into a hot spot of nightlife the pulsating music from the clubs and lounges mingles with the ocean whisper creating an atmosphere that's both energetic and soothing andab cornich is where the city comes to unwind it's a place to feel the pulse of Casablanca one wave at a time number five we have the impressive Morocco Mall this architectural Marvel is more than just a shopping destination it is a spectacle in its own right imagine getting lost in a sea of more than 300 stores ranging from high-end designer boutiques to affordable local Brands it's a Shoppers Paradise but that's not all the mall is also home to a phenomenal aquarium where you can Marvel at the diverse marine life or even take a deep dive if you're feeling brave and if you're traveling with kids or are young at heart there's an indoor Fairground that will send your spirits soaring whether you're indulging in a gastronomic adventure at one of the many eateries catching a movie at the cinema or just enjoying a leisurely stroll there's something for everyone at Morocco mall and remember Morocco mall is Where Shopping meets entertainment it's a destination that offers more than just retail therapy it's a place where memories are made is number six on our list is the opulent Royal Palace of Casablanca an architectural Marvel The Palace is a shining Jewel amidst the hustle and bustle of the city it's not just a residence of the king of Morocco when he visits the city but also a symbol of sovereignty a beacon of the country's Rich cultural heritage cloaked in mystery and Majesty the palace is a fusion of traditional Moroccan and modern architecture intricate designs Adorn its walls while Lush ver Gardens provide a Serene Oasis amidst the urban landscape every corner of the palace exudes Grandeur telling Tales of royalty power and the Timeless Allure of Moroccan craftsmanship now if you're thinking of popping in for a royal copper hold your horses the palace isn't open to the public but the exterior alone is worth a visit a chance to Marvel at its Splendor and perhaps snap a selfie or two with a palace guard the Royal Palace is a testament to Morocco's Rich Heritage coming in at number five is the bustling Central Market here the vibrancy of Casablanca comes alive in a symphony of sights sounds and scents it's a place where the city's heart beats with the rhythm of bartering voices and the clatter of Commerce the Central Market Is a gastronomic Wonderland that celebrates Morocco's culinary diversity it's a paradise for food lovers offering a smoggers board of local produce that bursts with color and flavor from the plump sun-kissed fruits to the crisp fresh veg vegetables your senses will be tantalized at every turn now let's dive into the seafood section The Catch of the Day is displayed with pride promising the freshest Seafood that the Moroccan Coast has to offer picture this succulent shrimps lustrous oysters and gleaming fish that leap straight from the ocean to your plate the Central Market is more than just a market it's a sensory Journey that captures the essence of Moroccan Cuisine the Central Market is where you can taste the true flavors of Morocco number four on our list is the Charming habo quarter this delightful area of Casablanca also known as the new Medina is a 20th century invention blending French colonial style with traditional Moroccan design it's like stepping back in time but with a unique twist nestled within the quarter's narrow winding streets you'll find an array of shops brimming with local crafts from ornate lanterns to vibr rugs and intricately designed leather goods there's a treasure to be found around every corner and don't forget to sample some of the delicious local pastries they're a sweet treat not to be missed yet despite the bustle of the shops the habo quarter maintains a tranquil atmosphere you'll find peaceful Courtyards stunning archways and quiet squares perfect for a moment's rest bite it's a place where the modern world seems to fade away replaced by the G rhythm of traditional Moroccan life the habo quarter is a slice of traditional Morocco in the heart of Casablanca coming in at number three is the stunning Casablanca cathedral in the Heart of the City this architectural gem truly stands out the cathedral also known as the Church of the Sacred Heart is a testament to Casablanca Colonial past with its construction completed in the year 1930 the cathedral's beauty is evident in its intricate stained glass windows each pain tells a story casting a myriad of colors around the interior as the sunlight filters through the Grandeur continues with the Towering Bell Tower reaching towards the heavens and providing a breathtaking view of the cityscape as you walk through the cathedral you're stepping through history it was once a vibrant place of worship then later transformed into a school and now stands as an iconic symbol of Casablanca diverse cultural heritage the Casablanca Cathedral with its its mix of European and Moroccan Styles is more than just a building it's a piece of art a slice of history and indeed a beacon of Tranquility in the bustling City number two on our list is the iconic Muhammad Viv square a place where the pulse of Casablanca beats the strongest this square is a testament to the city's vibrant spirit and Architectural Grandeur surrounded by a medley of beautiful buildings each a masterpiece in its own right the square showcases the city's Franco Moroccan style a blend of French Colonial and traditional Moroccan architecture the Square's centerpiece a majestic Fountain is a sight to Behold a symbol of life and renewal in the heart of a bustling City the sound of water gushing from the fountain mingles with the chatter of locals and the call of Street vendors creating a symphony that is quintessentially Casablanca day or night the square is alive with energy a meeting point for locals and a must visit for any traveler whether you're soaking in the grand of the architecture or immersing in the city's vibrant rhythms Muhammad V Square offers a slice of Casablanca that's hard to forget Muhammad VI square is the heart of Casablanca and finally at number one we have the legendary Rick's Cafe this iconic spot nestled in the heart of Casablanca is steeped in history and cinematic law its ties to the classic film Casablanca have made it a beacon for film Buffs and dreamers alike opened in 2004 the cafe as a a brilliant homage to the movie with its period decor and nostalgic Ambiance it's more than just a cafe it's a tribute to a bygone era a testament to the enduring Allure of the Silver Screen as you step through the doors you're greeted by the strains of the piano hearkening back to the famous scene where Sam plays as time goes by the atmosphere is palpable transporting you back to the 1940s immersing you in a world of romance and Intrigue Rick Cafe is a must visit for every traveler it's the perfect end to our Casablanca Journey so there you have it our top 10 must visit places in Casablanca we began our journey at the Majestic Hassan 2 mosque the world's third largest mosque with its a inspiring architecture we then dived into the past at the old Medina a Labyrinth of traditional Moroccan culture our journey continued along the aab Cornish where the city meets the ocean and then then we explored the Morocco Mall a Shopper's Paradise we were a struck by the Grandeur of the Royal Palace of Casablanca and the bustling Central Market's vibrant colors and flavors in the habo quarter we experienced a unique blend of French and Moroccan cultures while the Casablanca Cathedral showcased the city's Colonial past we then walked through the historic Muhammad fif square and finally we ended our tour at Rick's Cafe a tribute to one of Hollywood's great greatest Classics Casablanca a city of contrasts and surprises awaits you until next time happy travels

recommendations: Casablanca Morocco: 10 BEST Things To Do In 2024 (Travel Guide)

videos.title: Best Things To Do In Casablanca | Morocco Travel Guide 2025
videos.description:
Think Casablanca is all black-and-white nostalgia and Bogart one-liners? Think again. This is Morocco’s chaotic, soulful, art-deco-anarchist of a city—where Atlantic waves crash into ancient shrines, grilled sardines taste like religion, and rooftop jazz clubs bump elbows with beach bars. It’s gritty. It’s stylish. It’s real.

🌊 What’s Inside:
🕌 Hassan II Mosque – A seaside architectural flex you can tour
🌴 The Corniche – Where beach clubs meet waves and locals unwind
🐟 Derb Omar’s grilled sardines – No menu, no problem
📚 Habous Quarter – Faux-traditional, fully charming (with elite pastries)
👟 Maarif fashion hunt – Moroccan streetwear, sneaker gems, and indie finds
🍸 Rick’s Café – Tourist trap? Yes. Worth it? Also yes.
🎨 Villa des Arts – Where modern art meets Moroccan motifs
🧿 Sidi Abderrahman Island – Spiritual escape meets wave-battered wonder
🛍️ Markets, medinas, and a whole lot of mint tea madness

🌍 BONUS WATCH:
Love your coastal cities a little off-script? Check out our video on Africa’s Best Underrated Coastal Towns—surf, souks, and zero crowds.

👍 Like this if Casablanca just surprised you in the best way

tags: best things to do in casablanca
videos.transcript: [Music] There's a man in a linen suit sipping mint tea under a crumbling art deco archway, watching the Atlantic crash against a centuries old seaw wall. 5 minutes ago, he was bartering for saffron with a spice merchant. Now he's plotting whether to surf at A and Duban or sneak into a rooftop jazz club. No, this isn't a movie. This is Casablanca and it's not what you think. Sure, you came here expecting romance and Rick's cafe nostalgia, but Casablanca is not stuck in the 1940s. It's a living, chaotic, stylish beast that will seduce you, confuse you, and leave you wondering why you ever considered skipping it for Marrakesh. Stay with us because what's coming up will flip your idea of this city on its fezcoed head. [Music] Let's start with the crown jewel, Hassan II Mosque. It's not just massive, it's extra. Built partly over the ocean, this mosque's minouret rockets 210 m into the sky. That's taller than the Eiffel Tower without its hat. Non-Muslims can take a guided tour inside where the ceilings open up like a James Bond villain's lair, revealing cedar, marble, and that cool seab breeze that whispers, "You're definitely underdressed." From here, stroll the Cornesh, Casablanca's seaside prominade. Think Moroccan Miami minus the Kardashians. You'll find beach clubs, surfers, and enough dramatic ocean spray to film your own music video. This is where locals flirt, jog, and try to forget their rents due tomorrow. Now, you could have lunch at a flashy hotel. Or you could follow your nose to a smoky grill in Darb Omar, where a man with a singed mustache hands you the best grilled sardines of your life. No menus, no English, just a paper plate, a squeeze of lemon, and a mental note to Google. How to remove fish smell from hands. Time travel. Next, habis quarter. It looks old, but was actually built by the French in the 1930s to look traditionally Moroccan. Basically, orientalism in beige limestone. But don't let the faux vibes fool you. The bookshops here are fantastic, and the pastries at Bennis Pastry Shop will make you question your loyalty to Parisian croissants. You've never truly lived until you've had a gazelle horn that tastes like almondscented poetry. At this point, you're full of fish and sugar, so naturally, it's time to go shopping. Skip the touristy tat and dive into Marv, Casablanca's fashion district, where cool locals hunt for edgy Moroccan designers and sneakers rare enough to have their own bodyguards. But let's not ignore the elephant in the medina, Rick's Cafe. Yes, it's real. Yes, it's touristy. But come on, it's Casablanca. You kind of have to go. Need a break from all that urban swagger? Escape to Villa Desars, an unexpectedly tranquil modern art museum. Entry is usually free, and the contrast between traditional Moroccan motifs and avantgard installations feels like someone dropped bow house into a spice market. And just when you think Casablanca has no soul left to give, it drops a curveball. Sidi Abduraman Island connected to the mainland by a bridge. This spiritual site feels like a dream sequence. Whitewash shrines, crashing waves, and people deep in prayer. It's raw. It's real. And it's nothing like the rest of the city. So, what's the real best thing to do in Casablanca? Let the chaos carry you. Let the call to prayer echo over honking horns. Let your feet wander from crumbling colonial arcades into 21st century glass towers. Let go of the idea that this city should entertain you like a theme park. And let it challenge you, surprise you, even mess with you a little. Cuz Casablanca isn't here to play nice. It's here to play real. Still craving more off-script adventures along the continent's edge? Check out our video on the best African coastal towns to explore, where fishing villages meet surf breaks, spice markets back onto quiet beaches, and the crowds are always somewhere else. Now, back to our final reveal. You came here looking for the best things to do, but what you found was a city that doesn't try to impress you like a performer. It just is what it is. And the magic it's in letting go of the itinerary and giving into the madness. So yes, go to the mosque. Yes, sip overpriced cocktails at Rick's. But also get lost in the maze of Mare Central. Say yes to that questionable looking street sandwich. Learn three words of Daria and watch someone's face light up. Because in Casablanca, the best thing to do is everything you didn't plan for.

safetyNotes: But don't let the faux vibes fool you
